Package org.rodinp.internal.core

Class Openable

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.core.runtime.PlatformObject
org.rodinp.core.basis.RodinElement
org.rodinp.internal.core.Openable
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IOpenable, IRodinElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
RodinDB, RodinFile, RodinProject
public abstract class Openable extends RodinElement implements IOpenable
Abstract class for implementations of Rodin elements which are IOpenable.
See Also:
  • IRodinElement
  • IOpenable

  • Method Details

    • buildStructure

      protected abstract boolean buildStructure(OpenableElementInfo info, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor pm, RodinDBManager.OpenableMap newElements, org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource underlyingResource) throws RodinDBException
      Builds this element's structure and properties in the given info object, based on this element's current contents (reuse buffer contents if this element has an open buffer, or resource contents if this element does not have an open buffer). Children are placed in the given newElements table (note, this element has already been placed in the newElements table). Returns true if successful, or false if an error is encountered while determining the structure of this element.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • canBeRemovedFromCache

      public boolean canBeRemovedFromCache()

    • close

      public void close() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Closes this element and unloads it. Closing an element which is not open has no effect.

      Note: although IOpenable.close() is exposed in the API, clients are not expected to open and close elements - the Rodin database does this automatically as elements are accessed.

      Specified by:
      close in interface IOpenable
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs closing this element

    • closing

      public void closing(OpenableElementInfo info)
      This element is being closed. Do any necessary cleanup.

    • createElementInfo

      protected abstract OpenableElementInfo createElementInfo()
      Specified by:
      createElementInfo in class RodinElement

    • exists

      public boolean exists()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element exists in the database.

      Rodin elements are handle objects that may or may not be backed by an actual element. Rodin elements that are backed by an actual element are said to "exist", and this method returns true. For Rodin elements, it is always the case that if the element exists, then its parent also exists (provided it has one). Moreover, for any element which is not a stable file snapshot, the parent includes the element as one of its children. It is therefore possible to navigate to any existing Rodin element (except stable file snapshots) from the root of the Rodin database along a chain of existing Rodin elements. On the other hand, a stable snapshot file never shows up among the children of its parent element.

      The fact that an openable element exists gives no guarantee upon whether it can be opened. For instance, a malformed Rodin file may exist, while it can never be opened by the database (because it contains invalid XML).

      A call to this method might open the ancestors of this element, but will never open this element itself.

      Specified by:
      exists in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      exists in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      true if this element exists in the Rodin database, and false if this element does not exist

    • generateInfos

      protected void generateInfos(OpenableElementInfo info, RodinDBManager.OpenableMap newElements, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • getCorrespondingResource

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource getCorrespondingResource()
      Return my underlying resource. Elements that may not have a corresponding resource must override this method.
      Specified by:
      getCorrespondingResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the corresponding resource, or null if none
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getElementInfo

      public OpenableElementInfo getElementInfo(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Returns the info for this handle. If this element is not already open, it and all of its parents are opened. Does not return null.
      Specified by:
      getElementInfo in class RodinElement
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element is not present or not accessible

    • getOpenable

      public Openable getOpenable()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the first openable parent. If this element is openable, the element itself is returned. Returns null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getOpenable in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      getOpenable in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      the first openable parent or null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

    • getParent

      public Openable getParent()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the element directly containing this element, or null if this element has no parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getParent in interface IRodinElement
      Overrides:
      getParent in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      the parent element, or null if this element has no parent
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

      hasChildren in class RodinElement
      Throws:

    • hasUnsavedChanges

      public boolean hasUnsavedChanges()
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Returns true if this element is open and:
      • it has unsaved changes, or
      • one of its descendants has unsaved changes
      Specified by:
      hasUnsavedChanges in interface IOpenable
      Returns:
      true if this element is open and:
      • it has unsaved changes, or
      • one of its descendants has unsaved changes
      See Also:
      • IOpenable

    • isConsistent

      public boolean isConsistent()
      Subclasses must override as required.
      Specified by:
      isConsistent in interface IOpenable
      Returns:
      true if the element is consistent with its underlying resource, false otherwise.
      See Also:
      • IOpenable

    • isOpen

      public boolean isOpen()
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Returns whether this openable is open. If this openable doesn't exist, this method will always return false (as it can't be opened).

      Important Note: In the case of a Rodin project, this method returns whether the project contents have been loaded in the Rodin database cache. This is very different from telling whether the project is open or closed (as seen by the end-user). The latter information is rather reported by the IRodinElement.exists() method: an open project does exists, while a closed project is considered not to exist anymore.

      Specified by:
      isOpen in interface IOpenable
      Returns:
      true iff this openable is open
      See Also:
      • IOpenable

    • isReadOnly

      public boolean isReadOnly()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element is read-only. An element can be read-only for two reasons: either it corresponds to a resource which is read-only, or it is an internal element that belongs to a stable snapshot of a Rodin file, or the stable snapshot itself.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      isReadOnly in interface IRodinElement
      Overrides:
      isReadOnly in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      true iff this element is read-only

    • isSourceElement

      protected boolean isSourceElement()
      Returns true if this represents a source element. Openable source elements have an associated buffer created when they are opened.

    • isStructureKnown

      protected boolean isStructureKnown()
      Returns whether the structure of this element is known. For example, for a Rodin file that could not be parsed, false is returned. If the structure of an element is unknown, navigations will return reasonable defaults. For example, getChildren will return an empty collection.

      Note: This does not imply anything about consistency with the underlying resource contents.

      Returns:
      true if the structure of this element is known

    • makeConsistent

      public void makeConsistent(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Makes this element consistent with its underlying resource by updating the element's structure and attributes as necessary.
      Specified by:
      makeConsistent in interface IOpenable
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element is unable to access the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      See Also:
      • IOpenable

    • open

      public void open(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor pm) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Opens this element and all parent elements that are not already open. For Rodin files, the contents of the underlying resource is loaded into the database.

      Note: although IOpenable.open(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor) is exposed in the API, clients are not expected to open and close elements - the Rodin database does this automatically as elements are accessed.

      Specified by:
      open in interface IOpenable
      Parameters:
      pm - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs accessing the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      See Also:
      • IOpenable

    • openParent

      protected void openParent(Object childInfo, RodinDBManager.OpenableMap newElements, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor pm) throws RodinDBException
      Open the parent element if necessary.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • openWhenClosed

      protected OpenableElementInfo openWhenClosed(OpenableElementInfo info, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • parentExists

      protected boolean parentExists()
      Answers true if the parent exists (null parent is answering true)

    • resourceExists

      protected boolean resourceExists()
      Returns whether the corresponding resource or associated file exists

    • save

      public void save(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor progress, boolean force) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Saves any changes in this element to its underlying resource via a workspace resource operation. This has no effect if it has no unsaved changed.

      The force parameter controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system. If false is specified, this method will only attempt to overwrite a corresponding file in the local file system provided it is in sync with the workbench. This option ensures there is no unintended data loss; it is the recommended setting. However, if true is specified, an attempt will be made to write a corresponding file in the local file system, overwriting any existing one if need be. In either case, if this method succeeds, the resource will be marked as being local (even if it wasn't before).

      As a result of this operation, the element is consistent with its underlying resource.

      Specified by:
      save in interface IOpenable
      Parameters:
      progress - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      force - it controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs accessing the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This Rodin element is read-only (READ_ONLY)

    • save

      public void save(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor progress, boolean force, boolean keepHistory) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Saves any changes in this element to its underlying resource via a workspace resource operation. This has no effect if it has no unsaved changed.

      The force parameter controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system. If false is specified, this method will only attempt to overwrite a corresponding file in the local file system provided it is in sync with the workbench. This option ensures there is no unintended data loss; it is the recommended setting. However, if true is specified, an attempt will be made to write a corresponding file in the local file system, overwriting any existing one if need be. In either case, if this method succeeds, the resource will be marked as being local (even if it wasn't before).

      As a result of this operation, the element is consistent with its underlying resource.

      Specified by:
      save in interface IOpenable
      Parameters:
      progress - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      force - it controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system
      keepHistory - a flag indicating whether or not to store the current contents in the local history
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs accessing the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This Rodin element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
